user_query: I run a small mobile coffee shop serving busy moms around school drop-off. Please optimize my local site for on-page SEO using only the files in this workspace and produce both code changes and a concise report. Inputs (read these and base the work on their contents): - site/index.html - site/menu.html - site/schedule.html - content/business.yaml - content/keywords.csv - content/menu.csv Required changes to site/*.html (modify files in place under site/): 1) Titles and meta descriptions: - For each page (index.html, menu.html, schedule.html), set a unique (<= 60 characters) that includes the business name and at least one keyword from content/keywords.csv relevant to that page. - Set a unique <meta name="description"> (<= 160 characters) for each page that includes at least one keyword from content/keywords.csv and mentions at least one service area from content/business.yaml serviceAreas. - Avoid duplicate titles and descriptions across pages. 2) Structured data for the homepage (index.html only): - Insert a single JSON-LD <script type="application/ld+json"> block using schema.org type "CafeOrCoffeeShop" populated from content/business.yaml. Include: @context, @type, name, description, telephone, url, priceRange, servesCuisine, openingHours, areaServed (from serviceAreas), and address as a PostalAddress with streetAddress, addressLocality, addressRegion, postalCode, and addressCountry. 3) Image alt text: - For every <img> tag in site/*.html whose src basename matches a row in content/menu.csv (column image), set the alt attribute to start with the exact Item value from that row, followed by a short descriptor (you may incorporate the Description). Keep the final alt text reasonably short (<= 80 characters). Ensure all such images have non-empty alt text. 4) Internal linking: - Add a consistent top-level <nav> to all three pages that includes internal links to index.html, menu.html, and schedule.html with link texts "Home", "Menu", and "Schedule". Deliverables (create these under output/): - output/seo_changes_report.md: A human-readable summary per page listing: old vs new title and meta description text; the keywords chosen from content/keywords.csv for that page; confirmation that the nav was added; how many images had alt text updated on that page. For index.html, also list the JSON-LD fields included. - output/seo_summary.json: Machine-readable summary with an array of objects, one per page, each containing: {"page": "site/<name>.html", "title": "...", "title_length": <number>, "description_length": <number>, "keywords_used": ["..."], "images_with_updated_alt": ["<image filename basenames>"], "json_ld_present": true|false}. Constraints and notes: - Use business details only from content/business.yaml and keywords only from content/keywords.csv. Do not invent new service areas or contact info. - Titles and descriptions must be unique across pages and observe the length limits. - The JSON-LD must reflect the YAML fields and include all required fields listed above. - Do not remove non-SEO content; only add/update what’s needed for SEO. - Leave a record of all changes via the report and JSON summary. Output paths to create or modify: - Modify in place: site/index.html, site/menu.html, site/schedule.html - Create: output/seo_changes_report.md, output/seo_summary.json All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
